texto: It seems as if, for every dragon head that is lopped off, two more terrible appear. Seems so. But in truth, Life is gaining all the while. Brute force, such power as there seems to be in things, cannot stand against ideas which are eternal.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Edward Everett Hale: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “It seems as if, for every dragon head that is lopped off, two more terrible appear. Seems so. But…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, vida, verdade pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de verdade e ideas.

texto: Life seeks life and loves life. The opening of a catkin of a willow, in the flight of the butterfly, in the chirping of a tree-toad or the sweep of an eagle - my life loves to see how others live, exults in their joy, and so far is partner in their great concern.
